refnamediv.refpurpose: Record sessions with, and gate on, the platform trust authority

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: pam_platformd.so is the PAM interface to platformd-trustd.service8. It has two roles, selected by its arguments and the module type it is stacked as. It never verifies a credential itself and does not replace pam_unix8 or pam_systemd8.

As a session module with method=, it is an observer: on session open it submits a structured record (user, service, tty, declared method) to the trust authority over Varlink and returns success. The declared method is recorded as a declaration, never as a verified fact.

As an auth or account module with policy=, it is a gate: it asks the trust authority to evaluate the named policy for the caller's session and returns PAM_SUCCESS if the policy is satisfied and PAM_AUTH_ERR otherwise. It fails closed — if the authority cannot be reached, it denies. It authenticates nobody; a stack uses it to route on platform state. The gate is advisory: it reflects the observed platform state, not a hardware boundary.

listitem.para: Used in a session line. The declared authentication method to record, for example fingerprint-pam-declared.
Used in an auth or account line. The trust policy to evaluate, for example verified-boot. See platformd-trustd.service8 for the policy names.

refsect1.title: Module types provided
refsect1.para: The session type records session establishment (with method=). The auth and account types provide the gate (with policy=). Without a recognised argument the module returns PAM_IGNORE.

refsect1.title: Example
refsect1.para: Offer strong factors only on a verified boot, falling back to the password otherwise. Place this in the auth section of a PAM service that authenticates users — a screen locker, or polkit-1, which is where run0 and other polkit escalations authenticate (run0's own stack has no auth phase):
refsect1.programlisting: auth [success=ignore default=3] pam_platformd.so policy=verified-boot auth sufficient pam_fprintd.so auth sufficient pam_howdy.so auth sufficient pam_pin.so auth required pam_unix.so
refsect1.para: On a verified boot the gate returns success and the stack continues into the strong factors; otherwise it denies and default=3 jumps the three of them to the password. Editing a live stack can lock you out — test against a throwaway service with pamtester first, and keep a root shell open. The installed platformd-gate.example walks through both.
